description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a prominent hip roof in a muted beige color. The facade features a wide, covered porch supported by slender white columns, and the main entryway is recessed beneath the porch roof. Windows are arranged in a simple, functional pattern, with double-hung white-framed windows on each visible side. The exterior walls are clad in a uniform reddish-brown brick, giving the house a classic suburban appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and extends over the porch, providing shelter and architectural interest. The overall design is practical and unadorned, suitable for a residential neighborhood.
description: A two-story brick house with a hip roof, covered porch, and white-framed windows arranged simply on all sides.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide covered porch, main entryway, and two windows above the porch.
description_view_1: Side view with three white-framed windows and the hip roof extending over the porch edge.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain brick wall and two windows, one on each floor.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with four windows and a partial view of the porch roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry beneath a wide covered porch with slender columns and no visible steps or decorative elements.
